page: Microsoft Azure F48as_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Fasv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng, as well as PassMark CPU and physics tests. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with small-block cached reads and large-block writes scoring in the top 10%, while large-block uncached reads rank in the top 25%. Cryptographic and compression capabilities are also strong, with single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reaching top-tier levels.

For specialized workloads, the F48as_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance in Redis SET operations and small-to-medium LLM inference text generation. However, performance scales down to average when executing prompt processing and text generation on larger 70B LLM models, indicating a hardware limitation for massive model inference without dedicated GPU acceleration. This server is suited for database operations, web serving, and CPU-bound parallel processing tasks that benefit from dedicated physical cores and high clock speeds without requiring local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure F48as_v7 is an x86_64 server from the Fasv7 family, featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs operating at 4.5 GHz. It is configured with 192.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage or integrated GPUs. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, the server delivers top-tier performance in stress-ng single-core and multi-core benchmarks, as well as PassMark CPU tests. Its memory subsystem achieves top-tier write speeds and strong read bandwidth. While it excels in Redis operations, database tasks, and small-to-medium LLM inference, performance drops to average for large 70B LLM workloads. This profile makes the F48as_v7 highly effective for compute-intensive general-purpose workloads, web serving, and database operations requiring dedicated CPU resources.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F48as_v7 is a Fasv7-family server featuring a 48-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor and 192.0 GB of RAM. It delivers top-tier CPU and memory performance for database and compute-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F48as_v7 is a 48-core AMD EPYC server with 192.0 GB of RAM, delivering top-tier compute performance for database and web workloads.
tagline: A dedicated forty-eight core AMD EPYC server offering balanced memory and top-tier compute performance for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs at 4.5 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
Top-tier CPU performance in stress-ng and PassMark benchmarks
Strong memory bandwidth and high-throughput Redis database operations
Suited for web serving, database management, and medium-scale LLM inference
No local storage or integrated GPUs, relying on network-attached Premium Storage
